extracted_fields.medical_necessity_summary: Marcus is a 6-year-old male with Autism Spectrum Disorder (Level 2) presenting with significant deficits in social communication, restricted and repetitive behaviors, and sensory sensitivities that substantially impair daily functioning. He exhibits frequent self-injurious behaviors (head-banging, hand-biting) at an average rate of 12 times per day and elopement behaviors 3–5 times per school day, posing significant safety risks. ABA therapy at the requested intensity is medically necessary and consistent with Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin #0473 for ASD.

Marcus is a 6-year-old male with Autism Spectrum Disorder (Level 2) presenting with significant deficits in social communication, restricted and repetitive behaviors, and sensory sensitivities that substantially impair daily functioning. He exhibits frequent self-injurious behaviors (head-banging, hand-biting) at an average rate of 12 times per day and elopement behaviors 3–5 times per school day, representing a significant safety risk. ABA therapy at the requested intensity is medically necessary and consistent with Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin #0473 for ASD.
extracted_fields.primary_treatment_goal: Reduce self-injurious behaviors (head-banging, hand-biting) and elopement behaviors to improve safety and enable access to educational programming
